  1409→    # Sentinel markers for the Queen identity block in CLAUDE.md
  1410→    _QUEEN_SENTINEL_START = "<!-- HIVE-QUEEN-SESSION-START -->"
  1411→    _QUEEN_SENTINEL_END = "<!-- HIVE-QUEEN-SESSION-END -->"
  1412→
  1413→    def _queen_write_identity_files(self) -> tuple[Path, Path]:
  1414→        """Write Queen identity files for compaction persistence.
  1415→
  1416→        Returns (claude_md_path, instructions_path) for cleanup.
  1417→        """
  1418→        from .prompts import _load_template
  1419→
  1420→        queen_prompt = _load_template("queen")
  1421→
  1422→        # Write full instructions to .hive/ so Queen can re-read after compaction
  1423→        hive_dir = self.project_path / ".hive"
  1424→        hive_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
  1425→        instructions_path = hive_dir / "queen-instructions.md"
  1426→        instructions_path.write_text(queen_prompt)
  1427→
  1428→        # Write condensed identity anchor to .claude/CLAUDE.md (re-read every turn)
  1429→        claude_dir = self.project_path / ".claude"
  1430→        claude_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
  1431→        claude_md = claude_dir / "CLAUDE.md"
  1432→
  1433→        queen_block = (
  1434→            f"\n{self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_START}\n"
  1435→            "# HIVE QUEEN BEE — ACTIVE SESSION\n"
  1436→            "You are the Queen Bee coordinator. You do NOT write code — you plan, decompose, and monitor.\n"
  1437→            "Full instructions: `.hive/queen-instructions.md` — re-read if your context feels incomplete.\n"
  1438→            "Operational state: `.hive/queen-state.md` — re-read to recall what you were working on.\n"
  1439→            "Always use `hive --json` for CLI commands. The daemon runs in background.\n"
  1440→            f"{self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_END}\n"
  1441→        )
  1442→
  1443→        existing = claude_md.read_text() if claude_md.exists() else ""
  1444→        if self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_START not in existing:
  1445→            claude_md.write_text(existing + queen_block)
  1446→
  1447→        return claude_md, instructions_path
  1448→
  1449→    def _queen_cleanup_identity_files(self, claude_md: Path, instructions_path: Path):
  1450→        """Remove Queen identity files written for the session."""
  1451→        # Strip queen block from CLAUDE.md
  1452→        if claude_md.exists():
  1453→            content = claude_md.read_text()
  1454→            start = content.find(self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_START)
  1455→            if start != -1:
  1456→                end = content.find(self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_END)
  1457→                if end != -1:
  1458→                    end += len(self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_END)
  1459→                    # Consume trailing newline if present
  1460→                    if end < len(content) and content[end] == "\n":
  1461→                        end += 1
  1462→                    cleaned = (content[:start] + content[end:]).rstrip("\n")
  1463→                    if cleaned.strip():
  1464→                        claude_md.write_text(cleaned + "\n")
  1465→                    else:
  1466→                        claude_md.unlink()
  1467→
  1468→        # Remove instructions file
  1469→        instructions_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
  1470→
  1471→        # Remove state file (ephemeral per-session)
  1472→        state_file = self.project_path / ".hive" / "queen-state.md"
  1473→        state_file.unlink(missing_ok=True)
  1474→
  1475→    def _queen_claude(self):
  1476→        """Launch Queen Bee as an interactive Claude CLI session."""
  1477→        # Claude CLI refuses to launch inside another Claude Code session.
  1478→        # Since the queen is a top-level interactive session (not nested), clear the guard.
  1479→        os.environ.pop("CLAUDECODE", None)
  1480→
  1481→        # Write identity files for compaction persistence
  1482→        claude_md, instructions_path = self._queen_write_identity_files()
  1483→
  1484→        # Short system prompt — full instructions are in the file
  1485→        short_prompt = "You are the Hive Queen Bee coordinator. Read .hive/queen-instructions.md for your full instructions now."
  1486→
  1487→        claude_cmd = os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CMD", "claude")
  1488→        cmd = [
  1489→            claude_cmd,
  1490→            "--model",
  1491→            Config.DEFAULT_MODEL,
  1492→            "--append-system-prompt",
  1493→            short_prompt,
  1494→            "--allowedTools",
  1495→            "Bash(hive:*) Bash(git:*) Bash(ls:*) Bash(find:*) Bash(rg:*) Read Edit Write",
  1496→        ]
  1497→
  1498→        print("Launching Queen Bee TUI (Claude CLI)...\n")
  1499→
  1500→        # Use subprocess.run (not os.execvp) so we can clean up identity files on exit
  1501→        try:
  1502→            result = subprocess.run(cmd)
  1503→            sys.exit(result.returncode)
  1504→        except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1505→            pass
  1506→        finally:
  1507→            self._queen_cleanup_identity_files(claude_md, instructions_path)
  1508→
  1509→    def _queen_codex(self):
  1510→        """Launch Queen Bee as an interactive Codex CLI session."""
  1511→        # Write identity files for compaction persistence (instructions + state files).
  1512→        claude_md, instructions_path = self._queen_write_identity_files()
  1513→
  1514→        short_prompt = "Read .hive/queen-instructions.md for your full instructions now."
  1515→
  1516→        # Persistent instructions that survive long chats + compaction.
  1517→        # Keep this short and point at the durable instruction/state files.
  1518→        developer_instructions = (
  1519→            "You are the Hive Queen Bee coordinator. You do NOT write code; you plan, decompose, and monitor.\\n"
  1520→            "Full instructions: .hive/queen-instructions.md (read now; re-read after compaction).\\n"
  1521→            "Operational state: .hive/queen-state.md (re-read after compaction; update after significant actions).\\n"
  1522→            "Before creating issues/epics, output a human-readable plan for user review and wait for explicit approval.\\n"
  1523→            "Always use hive --json for Hive CLI commands."
  1524→        )
  1525→
  1526→        # Codex compaction prompt (used when the client compacts long threads).
  1527→        # Ensure summaries keep the queen's durable context pointers.
  1528→        compact_prompt = (
  1529→            "Summarize the conversation for continuity.\\n"
  1530→            "Preserve: user goals, key decisions, current plan/issues, and next steps.\\n"
  1531→            "Always include a reminder to read .hive/queen-instructions.md and .hive/queen-state.md after compaction."
  1532→        )
  1533→
  1534→        codex_cmd = os.environ.get("CODEX_CMD", "codex")
  1535→        sandbox = os.environ.get("HIVE_CODEX_QUEEN_SANDBOX") or getattr(Config, "CODEX_SANDBOX", "workspace-write")
  1536→        approval = os.environ.get("HIVE_CODEX_QUEEN_APPROVAL_POLICY") or getattr(Config, "CODEX_APPROVAL_POLICY", "never")
  1537→        cmd = [
  1538→            codex_cmd,
  1539→            "--sandbox",
  1540→            sandbox,
  1541→            "--ask-for-approval",
  1542→            approval,
  1543→            "-c",
  1544→            f'developer_instructions="{developer_instructions}"',
  1545→            "-c",
  1546→            f'compact_prompt="{compact_prompt}"',
  1547→            "--cd",
  1548→            str(self.project_path),
  1549→            short_prompt,
  1550→        ]
  1551→
  1552→        print("Launching Queen Bee TUI (Codex CLI)...\n")
  1553→
  1554→        try:
  1555→            result = subprocess.run(cmd)
  1556→            sys.exit(result.returncode)
  1557→        except FileNotFoundError:
  1558→            self._error("Codex CLI not found. Install `codex` and ensure it's on PATH, or set CODEX_CMD to the codex executable path.")
  1559→        except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1560→            pass
  1561→        finally:
  1562→            self._queen_cleanup_identity_files(claude_md, instructions_path)
  1563→

  1565→def _do_setup(project_path: Path, project_name: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
  1566→    """Write a default .hive.toml if one doesn't exist."""
  1567→    target = project_path / ".hive.toml"
  1568→    if target.exists():
  1569→        if json_mode:
  1570→            print(json.dumps({"config_exists": True, "path": str(target)}))
  1571→        else:
  1572→            print(f"{target} already exists.")
  1573→        return
  1574→    target.write_text(f'[project]\nname = "{project_name}"\n\n[hive]\nbackend = "claude"\nmerge_queue_enabled = false\n')
  1575→    if json_mode:
  1576→        print(json.dumps({"config_created": str(target)}))
  1577→    else:
  1578→        print(f"Created {target}")
  1579→
  1580→
  1581→def main():
  1582→    """Main CLI entry point."""
  1583→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Hive multi-agent orchestrator")
  1584→
  1585→    # Global options
  1586→    parser.add_argument("--db", default=None, help="Database path (default: ~/.hive/hive.db)")
  1587→    parser.add_argument("--project", default=None, help="Project directory (auto-detected from git)")
  1588→    parser.add_argument(
  1589→        "--json",
  1590→        action="store_true",

  1592→        help="Output JSON (for programmatic use)",
  1593→    )
  1594→
  1595→    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(

  1599→
  1600→    # create command
  1601→    create_parser = subparsers.add_parser("create", help="Create a new issue")

  1609→        help="Issue type (task, bug, feature, step, epic)",
  1610→    )
  1611→    create_parser.add_argument(
  1612→        "--model",
  1613→        help="Model to use for this issue (overrides global WORKER_MODEL)",
  1614→    )
  1615→    create_parser.add_argument(
  1616→        "--depends-on",
  1617→        dest="depends_on",
  1618→        action="append",
  1619→        help="Issue ID this depends on (can be repeated: --depends-on w-abc --depends-on w-def)",
  1620→    )
  1621→    create_parser.add_argument(
  1622→        "--tags",
  1623→        type=str,
  1624→        default=None,
  1625→        help="Comma-separated tags (e.g. refactor,python,small)",
  1626→    )
  1627→
  1628→    # list command
  1629→    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all issues")

  1631→    list_parser.add_argument(

  1633→        choices=["priority", "created", "updated", "status", "title"],

  1643→    list_parser.add_argument(

  1648→    list_parser.add_argument("--todo", action="store_true", help="Show only actionable issues (excludes done/finalized/canceled)")
  1649→    list_parser.add_argument("--assignee", help="Filter by agent assignee")

  1651→
  1652→    # show command

  1663→
  1664→    # review command

  1668→    # update command (hidden — advanced)

  1693→    # epic command (hidden — advanced)

  1727→    # logs command (hidden — advanced)

  1737→    logs_parser.add_argument("--issue", help="Filter by issue ID")

  1764→    # queen command

  1792→    # debug command

  1794→
  1795→    args = parser.parse_args()
  1796→
  1797→    # ── Project auto-detection + layered config ──────────────────────
  1798→    if args.project:
  1799→        project_path = Path(args.project).resolve()
  1800→        project_name = project_path.name
  1801→    else:
  1802→        project_path, project_name = detect_project()
  1803→
  1804→    # Load layered config: defaults → ~/.hive/config.toml → .hive.toml → env
  1805→    Config.load(project_root=project_path)
  1806→
  1807→    # Ensure ~/.hive/ directory exists
  1808→    Config.HIVE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1809→
  1810→    if args.command in ("setup", "init"):
  1811→        _do_setup(project_path, project_name, json_mode=args.json_mode)
  1812→        return
  1813→
  1814→    # Resolve DB path: CLI flag > config
  1815→    db_path = args.db or Config.DB_PATH
  1816→
  1817→    # Initialize database
  1818→    db = Database(db_path)
  1819→    db.connect()
  1820→
  1821→    # Create CLI
  1822→    cli = HiveCLI(db, str(project_path))
  1823→    json_mode = args.json_mode
  1824→
  1825→    try:

  1838→        elif args.command == "list":

  1850→        elif args.command == "show":

  1854→        elif args.command == "review":

  1857→        elif args.command == "update":

  1867→            )
  1868→
  1869→        elif args.command == "cancel":
  1870→            cli.cancel(args.issue_id, reason=args.reason, json_mode=json_mode)
  1871→
  1872→        elif args.command == "finalize":
  1873→            cli.finalize(args.issue_id, resolution=args.resolution, json_mode=json_mode)
  1874→
  1875→        elif args.command == "retry":
  1876→            cli.retry(args.issue_id, notes=args.notes, json_mode=json_mode)
  1877→
  1878→        elif args.command == "epic":
  1879→            cli.epic(
  1880→                args.title,

  1886→            )
  1887→
  1888→        elif args.command == "dep":

  1901→        elif args.command == "agents":

  1904→        elif args.command == "logs":

  1921→        elif args.command == "metrics":

1→"""Prompt templates for Hive agents."""
     2→
     3→import hashlib
     4→import json
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from string import Template
     7→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
     8→
     9→from .utils import CompletionResult
    10→
    11→# Filename for file-based completion signal
    12→RESULT_FILE_NAME = ".hive-result.jsonl"
    13→
    14→# Filename for notes file
    15→NOTES_FILE_NAME = ".hive-notes.jsonl"
    16→
    17→
    18→# Template cache: name -> template string
    19→_template_cache: Dict[str, str] = {}
    20→
    21→# Directory containing .md template files
    22→_PROMPTS_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "prompts"
    23→
    24→
    25→def _load_template(name: str) -> str:
    26→    """
    27→    Load a prompt template from prompts/{name}.md.
    28→
    29→    Templates are cached after first load.
    30→
    31→    Args:
    32→        name: Template name (without .md extension)
    33→
    34→    Returns:
    35→        Raw template string
    36→    """
    37→    if name not in _template_cache:
    38→        template_path = _PROMPTS_DIR / f"{name}.md"
    39→        _template_cache[name] = template_path.read_text()
    40→    return _template_cache[name]
    41→
    42→
    43→def get_prompt_version(template_name: str) -> str:
    44→    """Return a short hash of the prompt template file content."""
    45→    content = _load_template(template_name)
    46→    return hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    47→
    48→
    49→def build_retry_context(db, issue_id: str) -> Optional[str]:
    50→    """
    51→    Build retry context by querying previous failure events for an issue.
    52→
    53→    Args:
    54→        db: Database instance
    55→        issue_id: Issue ID to query events for
    56→
    57→    Returns:
    58→        Formatted retry context as markdown string, or None if no failures found
    59→    """
    60→    # Query for different types of failure events
    61→    incomplete_events = db.get_events(issue_id=issue_id, event_type="incomplete")
    62→    merge_rejected_events = db.get_events(issue_id=issue_id, event_type="merge_rejected")
    63→    stalled_events = db.get_events(issue_id=issue_id, event_type="stalled")
    64→
    65→    # Collect failure descriptions
    66→    failures = []
    67→
    68→    # Process incomplete events
    69→    for event in incomplete_events:
    70→        detail = event.get("detail", {})
    71→        if isinstance(detail, str):
    72→            try:
    73→                detail = json.loads(detail)
    74→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
    75→                detail = {}
    76→
    77→        reason = detail.get("reason", "Unknown reason")
    78→        summary = detail.get("summary", "")
    79→
    80→        if summary:
    81→            failures.append(f"**Attempt failed**: {reason} — {summary}")
    82→        else:
    83→            failures.append(f"**Attempt failed**: {reason}")
    84→
    85→    # Process merge_rejected events
    86→    for event in merge_rejected_events:
    87→        detail = event.get("detail", {})
    88→        if isinstance(detail, str):
    89→            try:
    90→                detail = json.loads(detail)
    91→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
    92→                detail = {}
    93→
    94→        summary = detail.get("summary", "Merge was rejected")
    95→        failures.append(f"**Merge rejected**: {summary}")
    96→
    97→    # Process stalled events (similar structure to incomplete)
    98→    for event in stalled_events:
    99→        detail = event.get("detail", {})
   100→        if isinstance(detail, str):
   101→            try:
   102→                detail = json.loads(detail)
   103→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
   104→                detail = {}
   105→
   106→        reason = detail.get("reason", "Agent stalled")
   107→        summary = detail.get("summary", "")
   108→
   109→        if summary:
   110→            failures.append(f"**Attempt stalled**: {reason} — {summary}")
   111→        else:
   112→            failures.append(f"**Attempt stalled**: {reason}")
   113→
   114→    # Return formatted context if any failures found
   115→    if failures:
   116→        failures_text = "\n".join(f"- {failure}" for failure in failures)
   117→        return f"""## Prior Attempts
   118→This issue has been attempted before. Previous attempts failed:
   119→{failures_text}
   120→Address these specific failure reasons. Do not repeat the same mistakes."""
   121→
   122→    return None
   123→
   124→
   125→def build_worker_prompt(
   126→    agent_name: str,
   127→    issue: Dict[str, Any],
   128→    worktree_path: str,
   129→    branch_name: str,
   130→    project: str,
   131→    completed_steps: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   132→    notes: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,
   133→    retry_context: Optional[str] = None,
   134→) -> str:
   135→    """
   136→    Build the worker prompt for an issue.
   137→
   138→    Args:
   139→        agent_name: Name of the agent
   140→        issue: Issue dict with title and description
   141→        worktree_path: Path to the git worktree
   142→        branch_name: Git branch name
   143→        project: Project name
   144→        completed_steps: List of completed step summaries (for epics)
   145→        notes: List of note dicts from other workers
   146→        retry_context: Optional retry context from previous failures
   147→
   148→    Returns:
   149→        Formatted worker prompt string
   150→    """
   151→    # Build context section
   152→    context_parts = [
   153→        f"- You are working in a git worktree at: {worktree_path}",
   154→        f"- Branch: {branch_name}",
   155→    ]
   156→
   157→    context = "\n".join(context_parts)
   158→
   159→    # Build completed steps section (for epics)
   160→    completed_section = ""
   161→    if completed_steps:
   162→        completed_section = "\n\n### Previous Steps (already completed)\n" + "\n".join(
   163→            f"{i + 1}. {step}" for i, step in enumerate(completed_steps)
   164→        )
   165→
   166→    # Build notes section (knowledge from other workers)
   167→    notes_section = ""
   168→    if notes:
   169→        note_lines = []
   170→        for note in notes:
   171→            category = note.get("category", "discovery")
   172→            content = note.get("content", "")
   173→            source = note.get("issue_id", "project")
   174→            note_lines.append(f"- [{category}] {content} (from {source})")
   175→        notes_section = "\n\n### Project Notes (from other workers)\n" + "\n".join(note_lines)
   176→
   177→    # Build retry section
   178→    retry_section = ""
   179→    if retry_context:
   180→        retry_section = f"\n\n{retry_context}"
   181→
   182→    template_str = _load_template("worker")
   183→    return Template(template_str).safe_substitute(
   184→        agent_name=agent_name,
   185→        project=project,
   186→        title=issue["title"],
   187→        description=issue.get("description", ""),
   188→        context=context,
   189→        completed_section=completed_section,
   190→        notes_section=notes_section,
   191→        retry_section=retry_section,
   192→        worktree_path=worktree_path,
   193→    )
   194→
   195→
   196→def build_system_prompt(project: str, agent_name: str, worktree_path: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
   197→    """
   198→    Build the system prompt for an agent session.
   199→
   200→    Args:
   201→        project: Project name
   202→        agent_name: Agent name
   203→        worktree_path: Path to worktree (if available, checks for CLAUDE.md)
   204→
   205→    Returns:
   206→        System prompt string
   207→    """
   208→    template_str = _load_template("system")
   209→    base = Template(template_str).safe_substitute(
   210→        agent_name=agent_name,
   211→        project=project,
   212→    )
   213→
   214→    result = base.rstrip()
   215→
   216→    # Inject project-specific CLAUDE.md if it exists
   217→    if worktree_path:
   218→        claude_md = Path(worktree_path) / "CLAUDE.md"
   219→        if claude_md.exists():
   220→            result += f"\n\n## Project Instructions\n\n{claude_md.read_text()}"
   221→
   222→    return result
   223→
   224→
   225→def assess_completion(
   226→    messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   227→    file_result: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   228→) -> CompletionResult:
   229→    """
   230→    Assess completion based on file-based result only.
   231→
   232→    Args:
   233→        messages: List of message dicts from OpenCode session (unused, kept for compatibility)
   234→        file_result: Optional parsed result from .hive-result.jsonl file.
   235→            If provided, used directly to construct CompletionResult.
   236→
   237→    Returns:
   238→        CompletionResult with success status, reason, and artifacts
   239→    """
   240→    # If we have a file-based result, use it directly
   241→    if file_result is not None:
   242→        status = file_result.get("status", "unknown")
   243→        summary = file_result.get("summary", "")
   244→        blockers = file_result.get("blockers", [])
   245→        artifacts_list = file_result.get("artifacts", [])
   246→
   247→        # Convert artifacts list to dict
   248→        artifacts = {}
   249→        if isinstance(artifacts_list, list):
   250→            for artifact in artifacts_list:
   251→                if isinstance(artifact, dict):
   252→                    art_type = artifact.get("type")
   253→                    art_value = artifact.get("value")
   254→                    if art_type:
   255→                        artifacts[art_type] = art_value
   256→
   257→        reason = ""
   258→        if status != "success" and blockers:
   259→            reason = "; ".join(blockers) if isinstance(blockers, list) else str(blockers)
   260→        elif status != "success":
   261→            reason = f"Worker reported status: {status}"
   262→
   263→        return CompletionResult(
   264→            success=(status == "success"),
   265→            reason=reason,
   266→            summary=summary,
   267→            artifacts=artifacts,
   268→        )
   269→
   270→    # No file result = worker didn't write completion signal = failure
   271→    return CompletionResult(
   272→        success=False,
   273→        reason="Worker did not write completion signal (.hive-result.jsonl)",
   274→        summary="",
   275→    )
   276→
   277→
   278→def read_result_file(worktree_path: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   279→    """
   280→    Read and parse a .hive-result.jsonl file from a worktree.
   281→
   282→    Args:
   283→        worktree_path: Path to the git worktree
   284→
   285→    Returns:
   286→        Parsed dict from the JSON line, or None if file doesn't exist or is invalid.
   287→    """
   288→    result_path = Path(worktree_path) / RESULT_FILE_NAME
   289→    if not result_path.exists():
   290→        return None
   291→
   292→    try:
   293→        text = result_path.read_text().strip()
   294→        if not text:
   295→            return None
   296→        # Read the first non-empty line (JSONL format)
   297→        first_line = text.split("\n")[0].strip()
   298→        return json.loads(first_line)
   299→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError, IndexError):
   300→        return None
   301→
   302→
   303→def remove_result_file(worktree_path: str) -> bool:
   304→    """
   305→    Remove the .hive-result.jsonl file from a worktree.
   306→
   307→    Args:
   308→        worktree_path: Path to the git worktree
   309→
   310→    Returns:
   311→        True if file was removed, False if it didn't exist.
   312→    """
   313→    result_path = Path(worktree_path) / RESULT_FILE_NAME
   314→    try:
   315→        if result_path.exists():
   316→            result_path.unlink()
   317→            return True
   318→    except OSError:
   319→        pass
   320→    return False
   321→
   322→
   323→def read_notes_file(worktree_path: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   324→    """Read .hive-notes.jsonl from a worktree. Returns list of note dicts, or empty list."""
   325→    notes_path = Path(worktree_path) / NOTES_FILE_NAME
   326→    if not notes_path.exists():
   327→        return []
   328→    try:
   329→        text = notes_path.read_text().strip()
   330→        if not text:
   331→            return []
   332→        notes = []
   333→        for line in text.split("\n"):
   334→            line = line.strip()
   335→            if line:
   336→                notes.append(json.loads(line))
   337→        return notes
   338→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
   339→        return []
   340→
   341→
   342→def remove_notes_file(worktree_path: str) -> bool:
   343→    """Remove .hive-notes.jsonl from a worktree. Returns True if file existed."""
   344→    notes_path = Path(worktree_path) / NOTES_FILE_NAME
   345→    if notes_path.exists():
   346→        notes_path.unlink()
   347→        return True
   348→    return False
   349→
   350→
   351→def build_refinery_prompt(
   352→    issue_title: str,
   353→    issue_id: str,
   354→    branch_name: str,
   355→    worktree_path: str,
   356→    agent_name: Optional[str] = None,
   357→    test_command: Optional[str] = None,
   358→) -> str:
   359→    """
   360→    Build the Refinery prompt for processing a merge.
   361→
   362→    Args:
   363→        issue_title: Title of the issue being merged
   364→        issue_id: Issue ID
   365→        branch_name: Git branch name
   366→        worktree_path: Path to the worktree
   367→        agent_name: Name of the worker agent (optional)
   368→        test_command: Preferred test command from queue metadata
   369→
   370→    Returns:
   371→        Formatted refinery prompt string
   372→    """
   373→    if test_command:
   374→        problem = (
   375→            "Perform full first-pass merge review and integration. "
   376→            f"Preferred test command: {test_command}. "
   377→            "You may run additional tests as needed."
   378→        )
   379→    else:
   380→        problem = "Perform full first-pass merge review and integration. Determine and run the appropriate tests."
   381→
   382→    worker_line = f"- **Worker**: {agent_name}" if agent_name else ""
   383→    test_step = (
   384→        f"Run tests: `{test_command}` (plus any additional coverage needed)" if test_command else "Determine and run an appropriate test suite"
   385→    )
   386→
   387→    template_str = _load_template("refinery")
   388→    return Template(template_str).safe_substitute(
   389→        issue_id=issue_id,
   390→        issue_title=issue_title,
   391→        branch_name=branch_name,
   392→        worktree_path=worktree_path,
   393→        worker_line=worker_line,
   394→        problem=problem,
   395→        test_step=test_step,
   396→    )
   397→

1→"""Main orchestrator for Hive multi-agent system."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import logging
     5→from contextlib import contextmanager
     6→from dataclasses import dataclass
     7→from datetime import datetime
     8→from enum import Enum
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→from typing import Any, Awaitable, Dict, List, Optional
    11→
    12→from .config import Config, WORKER_PERMISSIONS
    13→from .db import Database
    14→from .git import create_worktree_async, get_commit_hash, has_diff_from_main_async, remove_worktree_async
    15→from .merge import MergeProcessor
    16→from .utils import generate_id, AgentIdentity, CompletionResult
    17→from .backends import OpenCodeClient, make_model_config
    18→from .prompts import (
    19→    assess_completion,
    20→    build_retry_context,
    21→    build_system_prompt,
    22→    build_worker_prompt,
    23→    get_prompt_version,
    24→    read_notes_file,
    25→    read_result_file,
    26→    remove_notes_file,
    27→    remove_result_file,
    28→)
    29→from .backends import SSEClient
    30→
    31→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    32→
    33→
    34→def _exc_detail(e: BaseException) -> str:
    35→    """Return a non-empty, human-readable string describing an exception.
    36→
    37→    str(e) is empty for exceptions like asyncio.TimeoutError that carry no
    38→    message. In that case fall back to the exception type name, and if a
    39→    message is present prefix it with the type name for extra clarity.
    40→    """
    41→    msg = str(e)
    42→    name = type(e).__name__
    43→    if not msg:
    44→        return name
    45→    return f"{name}: {msg}"
    46→
    47→
    48→class CompletionTransition(str, Enum):
    49→    """Transition outcomes for completion handling."""
    50→
    51→    SKIP_TERMINAL_ISSUE = "skip_terminal_issue"
    52→    FAIL_BUDGET = "fail_budget"
    53→    FAIL_ASSESSMENT = "fail_assessment"
    54→    FAIL_VALIDATION_NO_DIFF = "fail_validation_no_diff"
    55→    SUCCESS_DONE = "success_done"
    56→    SUCCESS_CYCLE_NEXT_STEP = "success_cycle_next_step"
    57→    ERROR_COMPLETION_HANDLER = "error_completion_handler"
    58→
    59→
    60→class PostAction(str, Enum):
    61→    """Post-transition action for completion handler."""
    62→
    63→    TEARDOWN = "teardown"
    64→    CONTINUE_AGENT = "continue_agent"
    65→
    66→
    67→class EscalationDecision(str, Enum):
    68→    """Escalation routing decision after a failure."""
    69→
    70→    RETRY = "retry"
    71→    AGENT_SWITCH = "agent_switch"
    72→    ESCALATE = "escalate"
    73→    ANOMALY_ESCALATE = "anomaly_escalate"
    74→
    75→
    76→class StalledTransition(str, Enum):
    77→    """Transition outcomes for stalled-agent handling."""
    78→
    79→    FAIL_STALLED_IN_PROGRESS = "fail_stalled_in_progress"
    80→    FAIL_STALLED_TERMINAL = "fail_stalled_terminal"
    81→
    82→
    83→@dataclass
    84→class CompletionDecision:
    85→    """Decision payload from completion transition analysis."""
    86→
    87→    transition: CompletionTransition
    88→    result: Optional[CompletionResult] = None
    89→    terminal_status: Optional[str] = None
    90→    budget_tokens: Optional[int] = None
    91→    validation_original_summary: Optional[str] = None
    92→    next_step: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    93→
    94→
    95→class Orchestrator:
    96→    """Main orchestration engine for Hive."""
    97→
    98→    def __init__(
    99→        self,
   100→        db: Database,
   101→        opencode_client: OpenCodeClient,
   102→        project_path: str,
   103→        project_name: str = "default",
   104→        sse_client: Optional[SSEClient] = None,
   105→    ):
   106→        """
   107→        Initialize orchestrator.
   108→
   109→        Args:
   110→            db: Database instance
   111→            opencode_client: OpenCode HTTP client (or ClaudeWSBackend)
   112→            project_path: Path to the project repository
   113→            project_name: Name of the project
   114→            sse_client: Optional SSE client override (e.g. ClaudeWSBackend serves both roles)
   115→        """
   116→        self.db = db
   117→        self.opencode = opencode_client
   118→        self.project_path = Path(project_path).resolve()
   119→        self.project_name = project_name
   120→
   121→        # Merge processor
   122→        self.merge_processor = MergeProcessor(
   123→            db=db,
   124→            opencode=opencode_client,
   125→            project_path=project_path,
   126→            project_name=project_name,
   127→        )
   128→
   129→        # Track active agents
   130→        self.active_agents: Dict[str, AgentIdentity] = {}
   131→
   132→        # Reverse lookup maps for O(1) session_id and issue_id lookups
   133→        self._session_to_agent: dict[str, str] = {}  # session_id -> agent_id
   134→        self._issue_to_agent: dict[str, str] = {}  # issue_id -> agent_id
   135→
   136→        # SSE client for event monitoring
   137→        self.sse_client = sse_client or SSEClient(
   138→            base_url=Config.OPENCODE_URL,
   139→            password=Config.OPENCODE_PASSWORD,
   140→            global_events=True,
   141→        )
   142→
   143→        # Event handlers
   144→        self.session_status_events: Dict[str, asyncio.Event] = {}
   145→
   146→        # Track last SSE activity per session for lease renewal
   147→        self._session_last_activity: Dict[str, datetime] = {}
   148→
   149→        # Running flag
   150→        self.running = False
   151→
   152→        # Guard against double-handling: tracks agent_ids currently being
   153→        # processed by handle_agent_complete or handle_stalled_agent. Prevents
   154→        # interleaving across await points from causing duplicate processing.
   155→        self._handling_agents: set[str] = set()
   156→
   157→        # Degraded mode state
   158→        self._opencode_healthy = True
   159→        self._degraded_since: Optional[datetime] = None
   160→        self._backoff_delay = 5  # Initial backoff delay in seconds
   161→
   162→        # Cost guardrails
   163→        self._budget_paused = False
   164→
   165→        # Guard against TOCTOU race in main_loop: tracks issue_ids currently
   166→        # being spawned. Prevents duplicate spawn_worker calls when
   167→        # create_worktree_async yields control back to the event loop.
   168→        self._spawning_issues: set[str] = set()
   169→
   170→    def _setup_sse_handlers(self):
   171→        """Set up SSE event handlers."""
   172→
   173→        async def handle_session_status(properties):
   174→            session_id = properties.get("sessionID")
   175→            status = properties.get("status", {})
   176→            status_type = status.get("type")
   177→
   178→            if not session_id:
   179→                logger.warning(f"session.status event missing sessionID: {properties}")
   180→                return
   181→
   182→            # Any session activity = renew the lease for the associated agent
   183→            self._renew_lease_for_session(session_id)
   184→
   185→            # If session becomes idle, signal completion
   186→            if status_type == "idle":
   187→                event = self.session_status_events.get(session_id)
   188→                logger.info(
   189→                    f"Received idle session.status for session {session_id} "
   190→                    f"(event_exists={bool(event)}, mapped_agent={self._session_to_agent.get(session_id)})"
   191→                )
   192→                if event:
   193→                    event.set()
   194→                else:
   195→                    logger.warning(
   196→                        f"Idle status received for session {session_id} but no monitor event exists "
   197→                        f"(mapped_agent={self._session_to_agent.get(session_id)})"
   198→                    )
   199→
   200→        self.sse_client.on("session.status", handle_session_status)
   201→
   202→        async def handle_session_error(properties):
   203→            session_id = properties.get("sessionID")
   204→            if not session_id:
   205→                return
   206→            agent_id = self._session_to_agent.get(session_id)
   207→            if not agent_id or agent_id not in self.active_agents:
   208→                return
   209→            agent = self.active_agents[agent_id]
   210→            logger.error(f"Session error for {agent.name}: {properties}")
   211→            self.db.log_event(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, "session_error", {"session_id": session_id, "error": properties})
   212→            await self.handle_stalled_agent(agent)
   213→
   214→        self.sse_client.on("session.error", handle_session_error)
   215→
   216→        # Register permission event handler
   217→        self.sse_client.on("permission.request", self._handle_permission_event)
   218→
   219→    async def _handle_permission_event(self, event_data: dict):
   220→        """Handle permission request from SSE event — resolve immediately."""
   221→        try:
   222→            perm_id = event_data.get("id")
   223→            if not perm_id:
   224→                # If SSE event doesn't include full permission data,
   225→                # fetch pending permissions and resolve
   226→                pending = await self.opencode.get_pending_permissions()
   227→                for perm in pending:
   228→                    decision = self.evaluate_permission_policy(perm)
   229→                    if decision:
   230→                        await self.opencode.reply_permission(perm["id"], reply=decision)
   231→                        self._log_permission_resolved(perm, decision)
   232→                return
   233→
   234→            decision = self.evaluate_permission_policy(event_data)
   235→            if decision:
   236→                await self.opencode.reply_permission(perm_id, reply=decision)
   237→                self._log_permission_resolved(event_data, decision)
   238→
   239→            logger.debug(f"Handled permission event via SSE: {perm_id}, decision: {decision}")
   240→
   241→        except Exception as e:
   242→            logger.warning(f"Error handling permission event: {e}")
   243→
   244→    def _log_permission_resolved(self, perm: Dict[str, Any], decision: str):
   245→        """Log permission resolution event."""
   246→        session_id = perm.get("sessionID")
   247→        agent_id = self._session_to_agent.get(session_id) if session_id else None
   248→        issue_id = None
   249→
   250→        if agent_id and agent_id in self.active_agents:
   251→            agent = self.active_agents[agent_id]
   252→            issue_id = agent.issue_id
   253→
   254→        if issue_id and agent_id:
   255→            self.db.log_event(
   256→                issue_id,
   257→                agent_id,
   258→                "permission_resolved",
   259→                {
   260→                    "permission": perm.get("permission"),
   261→                    "patterns": perm.get("patterns"),
   262→                    "decision": decision,
   263→                },
   264→            )
   265→
   266→    def _renew_lease_for_session(self, session_id: str):
   267→        """Renew the lease for the agent associated with a session.
   268→
   269→        Called on any SSE activity from the session, proving the worker
   270→        is still alive and making progress. Uses LEASE_EXTENSION (not
   271→        LEASE_DURATION) so renewals grant a shorter window than the
   272→        initial lease.
   273→        """
   274→        now = datetime.now()
   275→        self._session_last_activity[session_id] = now
   276→
   277→        # Find agent for this session and extend its DB lease
   278→        agent_id = self._session_to_agent.get(session_id)
   279→        if agent_id and agent_id in self.active_agents:
   280→            agent = self.active_agents[agent_id]
   281→            try:
   282→                self.db.conn.execute(
   283→                    """
   284→                    UPDATE agents
   285→                    SET lease_expires_at = datetime('now', '+{} seconds'),
   286→                        last_progress_at = datetime('now')
   287→                    WHERE id = ?
   288→                    """.format(Config.LEASE_EXTENSION),
   289→                    (agent.agent_id,),
   290→                )
   291→                self.db.conn.commit()
   292→            except Exception:
   293→                pass  # Non-critical, best-effort
   294→
   295→    async def _reconcile_stale_agents(self):
   296→        """Bidirectional reconciliation on startup.
   297→
   298→        Four phases:
   299→        - Phase 0: Fetch live sessions from OpenCode server
   300→        - Phase 1: Reconcile DB agents with status='working' (ghost + live)
   301→        - Phase 2: Clean up orphan sessions (alive on server, no DB agent)
   302→        - Phase 3: Purge idle/failed agents (leftovers from previous runs)
   303→        """
   304→        # Phase 0 — Fetch live sessions
   305→        live_session_ids: set | None = None
   306→        try:
   307→            sessions = await self.opencode.list_sessions()
   308→            live_session_ids = {s["id"] for s in sessions}
   309→            logger.info(f"Fetched {len(live_session_ids)} live session(s) from backend")
   310→        except Exception as e:
   311→            logger.warning(f"Could not fetch live sessions from backend ({e}), falling back to DB-only reconciliation")
   312→
   313→        # Phase 1 — Reconcile stale DB agents
   314→        cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   315→            """
   316→            SELECT id, current_issue, worktree, name, session_id
   317→            FROM agents
   318→            WHERE status = 'working'
   319→            """
   320→        )
   321→        stale = cursor.fetchall()
   322→
   323→        if stale:
   324→            logger.info(f"Reconciling {len(stale)} stale agent(s) from previous run")
   325→
   326→        for row in stale:
   327→            agent_dict = dict(row)
   328→            agent_id = agent_dict["id"]
   329→            issue_id = agent_dict["current_issue"]
   330→            worktree = agent_dict["worktree"]
   331→            session_id = agent_dict["session_id"]
   332→
   333→            if session_id:
   334→                if live_session_ids is not None:
   335→                    # Authoritative: we know which sessions are alive
   336→                    if session_id in live_session_ids:
   337→                        # Session still running — abort + delete it
   338→                        await self.opencode.cleanup_session(session_id, directory=worktree)
   339→                        live_session_ids.discard(session_id)
   340→                    else:
   341→                        # Ghost agent — session already gone, just log
   342→                        logger.info(f"Agent {agent_id} is a ghost (session {session_id} no longer exists)")
   343→                else:
   344→                    # OpenCode unreachable — best-effort abort/delete
   345→                    await self.opencode.cleanup_session(session_id, directory=worktree)
   346→
   347→            # Mark agent failed
   348→            self.db.conn.execute(
   349→                "UPDATE agents SET status = 'failed', current_issue = NULL, session_id = NULL WHERE id = ?",
   350→                (agent_id,),
   351→            )
   352→
   353→            # Release the issue if still in_progress — but only if it
   354→            # hasn't exhausted its retry budget. Otherwise escalate to
   355→            # prevent an infinite spawn loop across daemon restarts.
   356→            if issue_id:
   357→                retry_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "retry")
   358→                agent_switch_count = self.db.count_events_by_type(issue_id, "agent_switch")
   359→
   360→                if retry_count < Config.MAX_RETRIES or agent_switch_count < Config.MAX_AGENT_SWITCHES:
   361→                    self.db.conn.execute(
   362→                        """
   363→                        UPDATE issues
   364→                        SET assignee = NULL, status = 'open'
   365→                        WHERE id = ? AND status = 'in_progress'
   366→                        """,
   367→                        (issue_id,),
   368→                    )
   369→                    self.db.log_event(
   370→                        issue_id,
   371→                        agent_id,
   372→                        "reconciled",
   373→                        {"reason": "stale agent from previous daemon run"},
   374→                    )
   375→                else:
   376→                    self.db.conn.execute(
   377→                        """
   378→                        UPDATE issues
   379→                        SET assignee = NULL, status = 'failed'
   380→                        WHERE id = ? AND status = 'in_progress'
   381→                        """,
   382→                        (issue_id,),
   383→                    )
   384→                    self.db.log_event(
   385→                        issue_id,
   386→                        agent_id,
   387→                        "reconciled",
   388→                        {"reason": "stale agent, retry budget exhausted — marking failed"},
   389→                    )
   390→
   391→            # Clean up worktree — but NOT if the issue is done with a
   392→            # pending merge queue entry. The merge processor still needs the
   393→            # worktree to run refinery review.
   394→            worktree_needed = False
   395→            if worktree and issue_id:
   396→                mq_row = self.db.conn.execute(
   397→                    "SELECT id FROM merge_queue WHERE issue_id = ? AND status IN ('queued', 'running')",
   398→                    (issue_id,),
   399→                ).fetchone()
   400→                if mq_row:
   401→                    worktree_needed = True
   402→                    logger.info(f"Preserving worktree {worktree} for pending merge of {issue_id}")
   403→
   404→            if worktree and not worktree_needed:
   405→                try:
   406→                    await remove_worktree_async(worktree)
   407→                except Exception:
   408→                    pass
   409→
   410→        if stale:
   411→            self.db.conn.commit()
   412→            logger.info(f"Reconciled {len(stale)} stale agent(s)")
   413→
   414→        # Phase 2 — Clean up orphan sessions (alive on server, no DB agent)
   415→        if live_session_ids is not None and live_session_ids:
   416→            # Collect all session_ids known to the DB (any status)
   417→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute("SELECT session_id FROM agents WHERE session_id IS NOT NULL")
   418→            db_session_ids = {row["session_id"] for row in cursor.fetchall()}
   419→
   420→            orphans = live_session_ids - db_session_ids
   421→            if orphans:
   422→                for session_id in orphans:
   423→                    await self.opencode.cleanup_session(session_id)
   424→
   425→                self.db.log_system_event("orphan_sessions_cleaned", {"count": len(orphans)})
   426→                logger.info(f"Cleaned up {len(orphans)} orphan session(s)")
   427→
   428→        # Phase 3: Purge idle/failed agents (leftovers from previous runs)
   429→        cursor = self.db.conn.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM agents WHERE status IN ('idle', 'failed')")
   430→        count = cursor.fetchone()[0]
   431→        if count > 0:
   432→            self.db.conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF")
   433→            self.db.conn.execute("DELETE FROM agents WHERE status IN ('idle', 'failed')")
   434→            self.db.conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
   435→            self.db.conn.commit()
   436→            logger.info(f"Purged {count} idle/failed agent(s) from previous runs")
   437→
   438→    def _rebuild_reverse_maps(self):
   439→        """Rebuild reverse lookup maps from current active_agents.
   440→
   441→        This is primarily for robustness and debugging. Under normal operation,
   442→        the maps should be kept in sync through spawn_worker and _unregister_agent.
   443→        """
   444→        self._session_to_agent.clear()
   445→        self._issue_to_agent.clear()
   446→
   447→        for agent_id, agent in self.active_agents.items():
   448→            self._session_to_agent[agent.session_id] = agent_id
   449→            self._issue_to_agent[agent.issue_id] = agent_id
   450→
   451→    def _register_active_agent(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
   452→        """Register an agent in active maps for session/issue lookup."""
   453→        self.active_agents[agent.agent_id] = agent
   454→        self._session_to_agent[agent.session_id] = agent.agent_id
   455→        self._issue_to_agent[agent.issue_id] = agent.agent_id
   456→
   457→    def _reassign_agent_context(self, agent: AgentIdentity, *, session_id: str, issue_id: str):
   458→        """Update an already-active agent to a new session/issue context."""
   459→        self._session_to_agent.pop(agent.session_id, None)
   460→        self._issue_to_agent.pop(agent.issue_id, None)
   461→        agent.session_id = session_id
   462→        agent.issue_id = issue_id
   463→        self._session_to_agent[session_id] = agent.agent_id
   464→        self._issue_to_agent[issue_id] = agent.agent_id
   465→
   466→    def _unregister_agent(self, agent_id: str):
   467→        """Remove an agent from active_agents and clean up reverse lookup maps.
   468→
   469→        Args:
   470→            agent_id: The agent ID to remove
   471→        """
   472→        agent = self.active_agents.get(agent_id)
   473→        if agent:
   474→            self._session_to_agent.pop(agent.session_id, None)
   475→            self._issue_to_agent.pop(agent.issue_id, None)
   476→            del self.active_agents[agent_id]
   477→
   478→    async def _shutdown_all_sessions(self):
   479→        """Mark agents as failed and release issues on shutdown.
   480→
   481→        Process cleanup (killing children) is handled by the backend's
   482→        __aexit__. This just updates DB state.
   483→        """
   484→        if not self.active_agents:
   485→            return
   486→
   487→        logger.info(f"Shutting down {len(self.active_agents)} active session(s)")
   488→
   489→        for agent_id, agent in list(self.active_agents.items()):
   490→            try:
   491→                self.db.conn.execute(
   492→                    """
   493→                    UPDATE agents
   494→                    SET status = 'failed', current_issue = NULL, session_id = NULL
   495→                    WHERE id = ?
   496→                    """,
   497→                    (agent_id,),
   498→                )
   499→                self.db.conn.execute(
   500→                    """
   501→                    UPDATE issues
   502→                    SET assignee = NULL, status = 'open'
   503→                    WHERE id = ? AND status = 'in_progress'
   504→                    """,
   505→                    (agent.issue_id,),
   506→                )
   507→            except Exception:
   508→                pass
   509→
   510→        try:
   511→            self.db.conn.commit()
   512→        except Exception:
   513→            pass
   514→
   515→        self.active_agents.clear()
   516→        self._session_to_agent.clear()
   517→        self._issue_to_agent.clear()
   518→        logger.info("All sessions shut down")
   519→
   520→    def _log_token_usage(self, agent: AgentIdentity, messages: List[Dict[str, Any]]):
   521→        """
   522→        Extract token usage from messages and log as 'tokens_used' event.
   523→
   524→        Args:
   525→            agent: Agent identity
   526→            messages: List of session messages from OpenCode
   527→        """
   528→        total_input_tokens = 0
   529→        total_output_tokens = 0
   530→        model = None
   531→
   532→        for message in messages:
   533→            metadata = message.get("metadata", {})
   534→            if metadata:
   535→                # Check if this message has token usage metadata
   536→                input_tokens = metadata.get("input_tokens", 0)
   537→                output_tokens = metadata.get("output_tokens", 0)
   538→                msg_model = metadata.get("model")
   539→
   540→                if input_tokens > 0 or output_tokens > 0:
   541→                    total_input_tokens += input_tokens
   542→                    total_output_tokens += output_tokens
   543→                    if msg_model and not model:
   544→                        model = msg_model
   545→
   546→        # Only log if we found some token usage
   547→        if total_input_tokens > 0 or total_output_tokens > 0:
   548→            detail = {
   549→                "input_tokens": total_input_tokens,
   550→                "output_tokens": total_output_tokens,
   551→            }
   552→            if model:
   553→                detail["model"] = model
   554→
   555→            self.db.log_event(
   556→                agent.issue_id,
   557→                agent.agent_id,
   558→                "tokens_used",
   559→                detail,
   560→            )
   561→
   562→    async def cancel_agent_for_issue(self, issue_id: str):
   563→        """Cancel the active agent working on an issue.
   564→
   565→        Aborts the opencode session, cleans up the agent and worktree.
   566→        Called when an issue is canceled while an agent is working on it.
   567→
   568→        Args:
   569→            issue_id: The issue ID that was canceled
   570→        """
   571→        # Cancel transition table:
   572→        # - CANCELLED_BY_USER -> wake monitor event, mark failed, log cancel, teardown+worktree cleanup
   573→
   574→        # Find the agent working on this issue
   575→        agent_id = self._issue_to_agent.get(issue_id)
   576→        agent = self.active_agents.get(agent_id) if agent_id else None
   577→
   578→        if not agent:
   579→            return  # No active agent for this issue
   580→
   581→        logger.info(f"Canceling agent {agent.name} (session {agent.session_id}) for issue {issue_id}")
   582→
   583→        # Signal the monitor_agent loop to stop waiting
   584→        event = self.session_status_events.get(agent.session_id)
   585→        if event:
   586→            event.set()
   587→
   588→        self._mark_agent_failed(agent.agent_id)
   589→
   590→        self.db.log_event(
   591→            issue_id,
   592→            agent.agent_id,
   593→            "agent_canceled",
   594→            {"reason": "issue canceled by user, session aborted"},
   595→        )
   596→
   597→        await self._teardown_agent(agent, remove_worktree=True)
   598→
   599→    async def start(self):
   600→        """Start the orchestrator."""
   601→        self.running = True
   602→        self._setup_sse_handlers()
   603→
   604→        # Start SSE/WS server in background FIRST — other init steps may need
   605→        # to create sessions (e.g. eager refinery), which requires the server.
   606→        sse_task = asyncio.create_task(self.sse_client.connect_with_reconnect())
   607→
   608→        # If the backend has a server_ready gate, wait for it before proceeding.
   609→        # IMPORTANT: if the event loop task dies before setting server_ready
   610→        # (e.g. missing binary / auth failure), don't hang forever.
   611→        if hasattr(self.sse_client, "server_ready"):
   612→            server_ready = self.sse_client.server_ready
   613→            while not server_ready.is_set():
   614→                if sse_task.done():
   615→                    exc = sse_task.exception()
   616→                    raise exc or RuntimeError("Backend event loop exited before becoming ready")
   617→                await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
   618→
   619→        await self._reconcile_stale_agents()
   620→
   621→        # Initialize merge processor (eager refinery session creation)
   622→        await self.merge_processor.initialize()
   623→
   624→        # Start permission unblocker in background
   625→        permission_task = asyncio.create_task(self.permission_unblocker_loop())
   626→
   627→        # Start merge queue processor in background
   628→        merge_task = asyncio.create_task(self.merge_processor_loop())
   629→        merge_task.add_done_callback(self._on_merge_task_done)
   630→
   631→        try:
   632→            # Run main loop
   633→            await self.main_loop()
   634→        finally:
   635→            self.running = False
   636→            # Abort all active opencode sessions before shutting down
   637→            await self._shutdown_all_sessions()
   638→            self.sse_client.stop()
   639→            # Cancel background tasks so we don't block on their long sleeps
   640→            for task in (sse_task, permission_task, merge_task):
   641→                task.cancel()
   642→            await asyncio.gather(sse_task, permission_task, merge_task, return_exceptions=True)
   643→
   644→    async def main_loop(self):
   645→        """Main orchestration loop."""
   646→        while self.running:
   647→            try:
   648→                # Check if OpenCode is healthy before scheduling work
   649→                if not self._opencode_healthy:
   650→                    # In degraded mode - check health with exponential backoff
   651→                    healthy = await self._check_opencode_health()
   652→
   653→                    if healthy:
   654→                        # OpenCode recovered
   655→                        degraded_duration = (datetime.now() - self._degraded_since).total_seconds() if self._degraded_since else 0
   656→                        self.db.log_system_event(
   657→                            "opencode_recovered", {"degraded_duration_seconds": degraded_duration, "backoff_delay": self._backoff_delay}
   658→                        )
   659→                        self._opencode_healthy = True
   660→                        self._degraded_since = None
   661→                        self._backoff_delay = 5  # Reset backoff
   662→                        logger.info(f"Backend recovered after {degraded_duration:.1f}s degraded mode")
   663→                    else:
   664→                        # Still unhealthy - wait with exponential backoff
   665→                        await asyncio.sleep(self._backoff_delay)
   666→                        self._backoff_delay = min(60, self._backoff_delay * 2)  # Cap at 60 seconds
   667→                        continue  # Skip scheduling and stall checks
   668→
   669→                # Per-run budget cap — stop spawning if total token spend exceeds limit
   670→                if Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_RUN:
   671→                    run_tokens = self.db.get_run_token_total()
   672→                    if run_tokens > Config.MAX_TOKENS_PER_RUN:
   673→                        if not self._budget_paused:
   674→                            logger.warning(f"Run budget exceeded ({run_tokens} tokens). Pausing new spawns.")
   675→                            self._budget_paused = True
   676→                            self.db.log_system_event("budget_paused", {"total_tokens": run_tokens})
   677→                        await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   678→                        # Still check for stalled agents even when budget-paused
   679→                        if self._opencode_healthy:
   680→                            await self.check_stalled_agents()
   681→                        continue
   682→
   683→                # Normal operation - check if we can spawn more agents
   684→                if len(self.active_agents) < Config.MAX_AGENTS:
   685→                    # Get ready work
   686→                    ready = self.db.get_ready_queue(project=self.project_name, limit=1)
   687→
   688→                    if ready:
   689→                        issue = ready[0]
   690→                        if issue["id"] in self._spawning_issues:
   691→                            await asyncio.sleep(1)  # Back off briefly while spawn completes
   692→                            continue
   693→                        try:
   694→                            # Try to claim and spawn worker
   695→                            await self.spawn_worker(issue)
   696→                        except Exception as e:
   697→                            # Check if the error suggests OpenCode is unhealthy
   698→                            if self._is_opencode_error(e):
   699→                                await self._enter_degraded_mode(str(e))
   700→                    else:
   701→                        # No ready work, wait before polling again
   702→                        await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   703→                else:
   704→                    # At capacity, wait
   705→                    await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   706→
   707→                # Check for stalled agents (only when healthy)
   708→                if self._opencode_healthy:
   709→                    await self.check_stalled_agents()
   710→
   711→            except Exception as e:
   712→                # Check if this is an OpenCode connectivity issue
   713→                if self._is_opencode_error(e):
   714→                    await self._enter_degraded_mode(str(e))
   715→                else:
   716→                    logger.error(f"Error in main loop: {e}")
   717→                    await asyncio.sleep(Config.POLL_INTERVAL)
   718→
   719→    async def spawn_worker(self, issue: Dict[str, str]):
   720→        """
   721→        Spawn a worker to handle an issue.
   722→        Always creates a new agent.
   723→
   724→        Args:
   725→            issue: Issue dict from database
   726→        """
   727→        issue_id = issue["id"]
   728→        self._spawning_issues.add(issue_id)
   729→        try:
   730→            await self._spawn_worker_inner(issue)
   731→        finally:
   732→            self._spawning_issues.discard(issue_id)
   733→
   734→    async def _spawn_worker_inner(self, issue: Dict[str, str]):
   735→        """Inner spawn logic, wrapped by spawn_worker's TOCTOU guard."""
   736→        issue_id = issue["id"]
   737→        agent_name = generate_id("worker")
   738→        model = issue.get("model") or Config.WORKER_MODEL or Config.DEFAULT_MODEL
   739→
   740→        # Create agent identity in database
   741→        agent_id = self.db.create_agent(
   742→            name=agent_name,
   743→            model=model,
   744→            metadata={"issue_id": issue_id},
   745→            project=self.project_name,
   746→        )
   747→
   748→        # Create git worktree (in executor to avoid blocking event loop)
   749→        try:
   750→            worktree_path = await create_worktree_async(str(self.project_path), agent_name)
   751→        except Exception as e:
   752→            self.db.log_event(
   753→                issue_id,
   754→                agent_id,
   755→                "worktree_error",
   756→                {"error": _exc_detail(e)},
   757→            )
   758→            self._delete_agent_row(agent_id)
   759→            return
   760→
   761→        # Atomic claim
   762→        claimed = self.db.claim_issue(issue_id, agent_id)
   763→        if not claimed:
   764→            # Someone else claimed it first, clean up worktree and delete agent
   765→            await remove_worktree_async(worktree_path)
   766→            self._delete_agent_row(agent_id)
   767→            return
   768→
   769→        # Create OpenCode session
   770→        session_id = None  # Track for cleanup on failure
   771→        try:
   772→            session = await self.opencode.create_session(
   773→                directory=worktree_path,
   774→                title=f"{agent_name}: {issue['title']}",
   775→                permissions=WORKER_PERMISSIONS,
   776→            )
   777→            session_id = session["id"]
   778→
   779→            # Update agent with session info
   780→            self.db.conn.execute(
   781→                """
   782→                UPDATE agents
   783→                SET session_id = ?,
   784→                    worktree = ?,
   785→                    lease_expires_at = datetime('now', '+{} seconds'),
   786→                    last_progress_at = datetime('now')
   787→                WHERE id = ?
   788→                """.format(Config.LEASE_DURATION),
   789→                (session_id, worktree_path, agent_id),
   790→            )
   791→            self.db.conn.commit()
   792→
   793→            # Create agent identity
   794→            agent = AgentIdentity(
   795→                agent_id=agent_id,
   796→                name=agent_name,
   797→                issue_id=issue_id,
   798→                worktree=worktree_path,
   799→                session_id=session_id,
   800→            )
   801→            self._register_active_agent(agent)
   802→
   803→            await self._dispatch_worker_to_issue(
   804→                agent=agent,
   805→                issue=issue,
   806→                model=model,
   807→                started_event_type="worker_started",
   808→                started_event_detail={
   809→                    "session_id": session_id,
   810→                    "worktree": worktree_path,
   811→                    "routing_method": "new_agent",
   812→                    "prompt_version": get_prompt_version("worker"),
   813→                    "model": model,
   814→                },
   815→            )
   816→
   817→        except Exception as e:
   818→            self.db.log_event(
   819→                issue_id,
   820→                agent_id,
   821→                "spawn_error",
   822→                {"error": _exc_detail(e)},
   823→            )
   824→            # Clean up the OpenCode session if it was created (best-effort —
   825→            # don't let cleanup failure prevent DB/worktree cleanup below)
   826→            if session_id:
   827→                await self._best_effort_cleanup(
   828→                    "spawn_session_cleanup",
   829→                    self.opencode.cleanup_session(session_id, directory=worktree_path),
   830→                )
   831→            # Clean up in-memory tracking (if agent was registered)
   832→            if agent_id in self.active_agents:
   833→                self._unregister_agent(agent_id)
   834→            # Mark agent as failed in DB
   835→            self._mark_agent_failed(agent_id)
   836→            # Clean up worktree and mark issue failed
   837→            await remove_worktree_async(worktree_path)
   838→            self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "failed")
   839→
   840→    def _gather_notes_for_worker(self, issue_id: str) -> Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
   841→        """Gather relevant notes to inject into a worker's prompt.
   842→
   843→        Combines epic-specific notes (if the issue is a step) with
   844→        recent project-wide notes, deduplicating by note ID.
   845→
   846→        Returns None if no notes are found (so build_worker_prompt skips the section).
   847→        """
   848→        seen_ids: set = set()
   849→        notes: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   850→
   851→        # Get epic-scoped notes if this is a step
   852→        issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   853→        if issue and issue.get("parent_id"):
   854→            for note in self.db.get_notes_for_epic(issue["parent_id"]):
   855→                if note["id"] not in seen_ids:
   856→                    seen_ids.add(note["id"])
   857→                    notes.append(note)
   858→
   859→        # Get recent project-wide notes
   860→        for note in self.db.get_notes(project=self.project_name, limit=10):
   861→            if note["id"] not in seen_ids:
   862→                seen_ids.add(note["id"])
   863→                notes.append(note)
   864→
   865→        return notes if notes else None
   866→
   867→    def _is_issue_canceled(self, issue_id: str) -> bool:
   868→        """Check if an issue has been canceled in the database."""
   869→        try:
   870→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   871→            return issue is not None and issue.get("status") == "canceled"
   872→        except Exception:
   873→            return False
   874→
   875→    async def _poll_session_idle(
   876→        self,
   877→        session_id: str,
   878→        worktree: str,
   879→        *,
   880→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   881→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   882→    ) -> bool:
   883→        """Poll opencode to check if a session has gone idle.
   884→
   885→        Fallback for when SSE events are missed (e.g., reconnect gap).
   886→        Returns True if the session is idle, False otherwise.
   887→        """
   888→        try:
   889→            status = await self.opencode.get_session_status(session_id, directory=worktree)
   890→            status_type = status.get("type") if isinstance(status, dict) else None
   891→
   892→            if status_type == "idle":
   893→                logger.info(f"Session poll detected idle for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, status={status})")
   894→                return True
   895→
   896→            if status_type in ("not_found", "error", None):
   897→                logger.warning(
   898→                    f"Session poll observed non-runnable status for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, status={status})"
   899→                )
   900→            else:
   901→                logger.debug(
   902→                    f"Session poll observed active status for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, status={status})"
   903→                )
   904→            return False
   905→        except Exception as e:
   906→            logger.warning(f"Session poll failed for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}): {e}")
   907→            return False
   908→
   909→    async def monitor_agent(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
   910→        """
   911→        Monitor an agent until completion.
   912→
   913→        Uses dual detection strategy:
   914→        1. SSE events: `session.status` → `idle` sets an asyncio.Event immediately
   915→        2. Polling fallback: every check_interval, polls `get_session_status` directly
   916→           to catch idle transitions that were missed by SSE (reconnect gaps, etc.)
   917→
   918→        After idle is detected, reads the result file for structured completion data.
   919→
   920→        Also checks periodically if the issue was canceled, and if so,
   921→        aborts the session immediately.
   922→
   923→        Args:
   924→            agent: Agent identity
   925→        """
   926→        # Snapshot the session_id we're monitoring. The agent object may be
   927→        # mutated by cycle_agent_to_next_step during epic processing,
   928→        # and we must clean up OUR session's event, not the new one.
   929→        my_session_id = agent.session_id
   930→        try:
   931→            event = self.session_status_events.get(my_session_id)
   932→            if not event:
   933→                logger.warning(
   934→                    f"Monitor started without session event for session {my_session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id})"
   935→                )
   936→                return
   937→
   938→            # Record initial activity
   939→            self._session_last_activity[my_session_id] = datetime.now()
   940→
   941→            # Poll loop: check for completion or inactivity
   942→            check_interval = min(30, Config.LEASE_DURATION // 4)
   943→            completion_detected_via = "unknown"
   944→            logger.info(
   945→                f"Starting monitor for session {my_session_id} "
   946→                f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, check_interval={check_interval}s)"
   947→            )
   948→            while True:
   949→                try:
   950→                    await asyncio.wait_for(event.wait(), timeout=check_interval)
   951→                    # Event was set — could be idle (done) or canceled
   952→                    # Check if canceled before assessing completion
   953→                    if self._is_issue_canceled(agent.issue_id):
   954→                        # Issue was canceled while agent was working.
   955→                        # cancel_agent_for_issue already handled cleanup + set the event.
   956→                        logger.info(
   957→                            f"Monitor exiting due to cancellation for session {my_session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id})"
   958→                        )
   959→                        return
   960→                    completion_detected_via = "event"
   961→                    break
   962→                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   963→                    logger.debug(
   964→                        f"Monitor timeout waiting for idle event for session {my_session_id}; "
   965→                        f"polling fallback (event_set={event.is_set()}, current_agent_session={agent.session_id})"
   966→                    )
   967→                    # Check if the issue was canceled
   968→                    if self._is_issue_canceled(agent.issue_id):
   969→                        await self.cancel_agent_for_issue(agent.issue_id)
   970→                        return
   971→
   972→                    # Polling fallback: directly check if the session went idle.
   973→                    # This catches cases where the SSE event was missed.
   974→                    if await self._poll_session_idle(
   975→                        my_session_id,
   976→                        agent.worktree,
   977→                        agent_id=agent.agent_id,
   978→                        issue_id=agent.issue_id,
   979→                    ):
   980→                        completion_detected_via = "poll"
   981→                        break
   982→
   983→                    # Check if there's been recent activity
   984→                    last_activity = self._session_last_activity.get(my_session_id, datetime.now())
   985→                    elapsed = (datetime.now() - last_activity).total_seconds()
   986→
   987→                    if elapsed > Config.LEASE_DURATION:
   988→                        # No activity for full lease duration — truly stalled
   989→                        await self.handle_stalled_agent(agent)
   990→                        return
   991→                    # Otherwise, keep waiting — worker is active
   992→
   993→            # AFTER idle detected, read result file for structured data
   994→            file_result = read_result_file(agent.worktree)
   995→            if file_result is None:
   996→                result_path = Path(agent.worktree) / ".hive-result.jsonl"
   997→                exists = result_path.exists()
   998→                size = None
   999→                mtime = None
  1000→                if exists:
  1001→                    try:
  1002→                        stat = result_path.stat()
  1003→                        size = stat.st_size
  1004→                        mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat()
  1005→                    except OSError as e:
  1006→                        logger.warning(f"Failed to stat result file for session {my_session_id}: {e}")
  1007→                logger.error(
  1008→                    f"Completion signal missing after idle detection for session {my_session_id} "
  1009→                    f"(detected_via={completion_detected_via}, current_agent_session={agent.session_id}, "
  1010→                    f"result_file={result_path}, exists={exists}, size={size}, mtime={mtime})"
  1011→                )
  1012→
  1013→            # Agent finished, assess completion
  1014→            await self.handle_agent_complete(agent, file_result=file_result)
  1015→
  1016→        except Exception as e:
  1017→            logger.exception(f"Monitor error for session {my_session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}): {e}")
  1018→            self.db.log_event(
  1019→                agent.issue_id,
  1020→                agent.agent_id,
  1021→                "monitor_error",
  1022→                {"error": str(e)},
  1023→            )
  1024→        finally:
  1025→            # Clean up using the snapshotted session_id, not agent.session_id
  1026→            # which may have been mutated by cycle_agent_to_next_step.
  1027→            logger.debug(
  1028→                f"Monitor cleanup for session {my_session_id} "
  1029→                f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, "
  1030→                f"event_present={my_session_id in self.session_status_events})"
  1031→            )
  1032→            if my_session_id in self.session_status_events:
  1033→                del self.session_status_events[my_session_id]
  1034→            self._session_last_activity.pop(my_session_id, None)
  1035→
  1036→    async def _cleanup_session(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
  1037→        """Abort and delete an agent's opencode session.
  1038→
  1039→        Called after agent completion or failure to ensure the session
  1040→        does not linger and consume tokens.
  1041→        """
  1042→        logger.info(f"Cleaning up session {agent.session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, worktree={agent.worktree})")
  1043→        await self.opencode.cleanup_session(agent.session_id, directory=agent.worktree)
  1044→
  1045→    def _mark_agent_failed(self, agent_id: str):
  1046→        """Mark an agent failed in DB and clear issue/session references."""
  1047→        self.db.conn.execute(
  1048→            """
  1049→            UPDATE agents
  1050→            SET status = 'failed',
  1051→                current_issue = NULL,
  1052→                session_id = NULL
  1053→            WHERE id = ?
  1054→            """,
  1055→            (agent_id,),
  1056→        )
  1057→        self.db.conn.commit()
  1058→
  1059→    def _release_issue(self, issue_id: str):
  1060→        """Release an issue back to the open queue."""
  1061→        self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "open")
  1062→        self.db.conn.execute("UPDATE issues SET assignee = NULL WHERE id = ?", (issue_id,))
  1063→        self.db.conn.commit()
  1064→
  1065→    def _delete_agent_row(self, agent_id: str):
  1066→        """Delete agent row for early spawn-orphan cleanup paths."""
  1067→        self.db.conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF")
  1068→        try:
  1069→            self.db.conn.execute("DELETE FROM agents WHERE id = ?", (agent_id,))
  1070→        finally:
  1071→            self.db.conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
  1072→        self.db.conn.commit()
  1073→
